ops.text:
RECOVERY RETURN (2026-07-19, separate lane; $0 spend — no re-voting, disk-only reconstruction). The prior lane appended its MID-RUN CHECKPOINT ~09:34 PDT and died before emitting a structured return.

DISK STATE (authoritative):
- out/votes_control_r1.jsonl, out/votes_control_r2.jsonl: BOTH present, 80 rows each, 0 null categories. vote.log shows both healthy (judged=80 failed=0). control_r2 finished AFTER the checkpoint was written (checkpoint said 1/8; disk is 2/8).
- E8 verified via triage table: clone_control_r1.db holds role e16_control_r1 (80 verdicts); clone_control_r2.db holds e16_control_r2 (80); each on its own fresh clone. No shared cache.
- clone_length_r1.db was pre-created (cp -c) but holds ZERO e16 verdicts — a genuine E8 NO-RUN. No votes_length/position/style.jsonl exist anywhere.
- vote.py process gone; no DONE marker in vote.log; no vote_summary.json; finalizer never launched (no finalizer.log); no results.json/summary.txt. The bg process died after control r2 — the 6 perturbation passes (length/position/style ×2) NEVER RAN.

HONEST RESULT — 2 of 8 passes complete (CONTROL only). Perturbation arms did not run (died, not descoped). analyze.py cannot run (line 37 requires all factors present), so I computed the control-only baseline directly:

CONTROL test-retest NOISE FLOOR (N=80, sonnet, frozen summary context):
- binary is_steering flip = 0.075 ± 0.029 (6/80 rows disagree r1 vs r2)
- 11-way category flip   = 0.125 ± 0.037 (10/80 rows disagree)
- is_steering=1 count: r1=54/80, r2=48/80 (net −6 positives on the retest)
- category flips concentrate in the ambiguous-directive cluster: unwanted_action 2/5, operational_directive 2/7; direction 0/18 and style_violation/premature 0/n are stable. Most category flips are steering↔noise boundary crossings (e.g. wrong_approach→new_task, direction→operational_directive).

MATERIALLY INCOMPLETE: the PRIMARY deliverable — per-factor flip-vs-control table + flagged-factor set for E19 — is UNPRODUCIBLE (no perturbation votes). Paraphrase fidelity spot-check (10/10 PASS) was done up-front but the style arm never voted, so it is unused.

INSTRUMENT-CARD OBSERVATION (survives): the pre-registered >5pp flag rule sits BELOW the instrument's own category noise floor (12.5%, SE 3.7pp) and at/near the binary floor (7.5%). At N=80 with matched-replicate netting, a real 5pp marginal factor effect would be near the test-retest SE band — E16's power at this N is thin; a completion pass should note this or raise N/replicates.

SPEND: this recovery lane ~$0 (disk reads + vote comparisons only). Prior lane sunk cost ≈ $2.9 (paraphrases opus ~$1.0; control r1 sonnet ~$0.9; control r2 sonnet ~$0.9; smoke ~$0.13). Remaining to complete the 6 perturbation passes ≈ $5.4–6 (sonnet, ~$0.9/pass); paraphrases already authored so no re-spend there. vote.py is resume-aware (skips the 2 done control passes) and analyze.py auto-produces results.json/summary.txt once all 8 JSONL exist — resume command in the checkpoint above still valid.

Recovered baseline written to out/recovered_control_results.json (attached).
